quote_of_day.text:
>>4512
kek my parents are always obsessed with civil services examinations without realising that I'm a 24 yo bum and my career gap is increasing

might as well have to pull the plug and take whatever job i can get at this point
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of political disillusionment moved over the board this morning, as Anon expressed frustration with the BJP, sparking a debate that drew several responses.

The system's centre was marked by claims of incompetence and accusations of pretended right-wing ideology.

A scattered high-pressure ridge of physical discussions formed later, marked by casual references to 'rolling' and other bodily attributes.

This gave way to a weak front of regional identity discussions, as an anon enquired about the presence of others from Kerala and sparked a few comments on the state's economic and cultural distinctiveness.

A brief but intense downpour of casteist and communalist remarks accompanied a comment about GOBBAR, prompting several replies that attempted to counter or ridicule the original statement.

The board's atmosphere remained charged, with little sign of clearing in the near term.

articles.title: Anon Expresses Disillusionment with BJP, Sparking Debate
articles.body:
Anon posted a scathing critique of the BJP, expressing disillusionment with the party's performance over the years.

The poster, who claimed to be a Hindu, stated that they initially supported the BJP's win in 2014 but have since grown to hate the party due to its perceived incompetence and fake right-wing ideology.

Replies to the OP were varied, with one anon suggesting that voting for Congress might not be the solution, as they may try to help Muslims 'rise up in rebellion against BJP.' Another anon argued that India's problems are not with its leaders but with its people, stating that it is 'in the nature of the pajeet to live and build a miserable life.' The discussion took a darker turn with some anons making inflammatory comments about Muslims and suggesting violent solutions to the country's problems.

One anon advocated for a strict pro-nation dictatorship with death sentences for corruption, citing Germany's innovations under Hitler as an example.

Others made derogatory comments about Muslims, with one referring to them as 'disgusting vermin.' Some anons also questioned the BJP's commitment to its ideology, with one pointing out that the party's actions seem to contradict its right-wing stance.

Another anon criticized the BJP for its handling of cow protection, citing a report that the party needs revenue from beef exports.

The thread also saw anons exchanging personal attacks and insults, with one anon telling another to 're-evaluate your life bro' for using derogatory language.

The discussion remained highly critical of the BJP and Modi, with many anons expressing frustration with the current state of the country and its politics.

The conversation also touched on the idea of India being 'recoverable,' with one anon responding that India was 'always a shithole.' The thread ended with anons continuing to exchange views and insults, with no clear resolution or consensus in sight.

articles.title: Anon Discusses Physical Attributes and 'Rolling'
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying 'rollin''. One respondent noted that some people, specifically 'Redditbhangis', allegedly cannot understand concepts like 'rolling' and 'quads'.

They described two physical types, associating the left with a curvier figure and a 'more majestic ass', and the right with a more youthful appearance, suiting temporary relations. Another poster chimed in, agreeing with the assessment.

They claimed the individuals in question are likely Latina. A respondent countered, expressing skepticism that Indian women naturally have both a curvy figure and a slim waist. One anon mentioned retiring for the night, suggesting others create content for later.

A respondent jokingly asked another to complete their homework. Anon also discussed older internet practices, like 'gobbaring'. Some posters made general statements about their observations of Indian women and their physical attributes.

One poster noted they have seen less obesity in younger generations. The conversation also involved some personal remarks and jokes. A comment was made about the South Indian perspective on women's physical attributes.

articles.title: Kerala's Economic and Cultural Identity
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread with a greeting to other Malayali anons. One respondent expressed concern about the influence of "gujjew" and stated that while India is not communist, its government has socialist policies.

Another anon complained about Kerala being perceived as communist and suggested allowing more capitalism to prevent migration to the Gulf countries.

A discussion on Bangalore and Dubai ensued, with one anon stating that Bangalore cannot compete with Dubai in terms of "hoeflation." Another respondent called out a perceived hypocrite, labeling them a "subhuman Dravidian" who migrates to "Aryan cities" for livelihood.

Some anons exchanged comments in Malayalam. One wrote that certain people will always migrate, while another made a cryptic comment about Kerala.

The thread also touched on the topic of monsoon status, with anons asking and responding to questions about the weather. One anon jokingly asked if anyone knew where the purebred Keralites were.

The conversation was marked by disagreements and jokes about economic systems, migration, and cultural identity.

articles.title: Anon sparks debate with GOBBAR comment
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion with a post saying "GOBBAR is upset ahahhahaha" and addressed "TO THE ARYAN MAN," suggesting they "BURN THE GOBAR IN THE YAGNA, AND RIGHTFULLY CLAIM YOUR GORIMEM." Another anon responded, calling it "tribal warfare" and stating that "diaspora's dead" and "racemixed kids are sterile and crazy." This drew several replies, including one that simply said, "If that's not G-d's 'fuck off' signal I don't know what is." The conversation also involved a link to a UC Davis article about biracial Asian Americans and mental health.

Anon questioned the relevance of the link to race mixing, saying "What does picrel have to do with race mixing?"
